summary: The job failed when trying to push a branch to the repository: git push returned a 403 permission error for the nextjs-bot account.
root_cause: git push failed because the remote rejected the push with 'Permission to vercel/next.js.git denied to nextjs-bot' (HTTP 403) when scripts/sync-react.js attempted to push branch update/react/19.3.0-canary-142cfde8-20260422.

evidence.quote: Error: Command failed with exit code 128 (Unknown system error -128): git push origin update/react/19.3.0-canary-142cfde8-20260422
evidence.reason: Shows the failing command was a git push of the branch update/react/19.3.0-canary-142cfde8-20260422 and that it exited with code 128.

evidence.quote: remote: Permission to vercel/next.js.git denied to nextjs-bot.
evidence.reason: Indicates the remote explicitly denied permission for the nextjs-bot account when attempting to push.

evidence.quote: fatal: unable to access 'https://github.com/vercel/next.js/': The requested URL returned error: 403
evidence.reason: Shows the HTTP 403 response from GitHub confirming an authentication/permission problem.
Identifies the script (scripts/sync-react.js) and location that attempted the git push.
Shows a GITHUB_TOKEN was present in the job environment, implying the push attempted to use provided credentials but still lacked permission.

suggested_fix: Ensure the credentials used for pushing have write access to vercel/next.js: update or replace the token used by the workflow (GITHUB_TOKEN or a PAT) so it grants repository push permissions to the account performing the push (nextjs-bot), or run the sync script with an account that has push rights. Verify actions/checkout and any git remote configuration use the correct token and permissions, then re-run the job.
